text.Rotherham: Meshezabel, Zadok, Jaddua;
text.KJV: 10:21 Meshezabeel, Zadok, Iaddua,
text.DRV: 10:21 Mesizabel, Sadoc, Iedua.

text.NRSV: Pelatiah, Hanan, Anaiah,
text.Rotherham: Pelatiah, Hanan, Anaiah;
text.KJV: 10:22 Pelatiah, Hanan, Anaiah,
text.DRV: 10:22 Pheltia, Hanan, Anai,

text.NRSV: Hoshea, Hananiah, Hasshub,
text.Rotherham: Hoshea, Hananiah, Hasshub;
text.KJV: 10:23 Hoshea, Hananiah, Hashub,
text.DRV: 10:23 Osee, Hanania, Hasub,

text.NRSV: Hallohesh, Pilha, Shobek,
text.Rotherham: Hallohesh, Pilha, Shobek;
text.KJV: 10:24 Hallohesh, Pileha, Shobek,
text.DRV: 10:24 Alohes, Phalea, Sobec,

text.NRSV: Rehum, Hashabnah, Maaseiah,
text.Rotherham: Rehum, Hashabnah, Maaseiah;
text.KJV: 10:25 Rehum, Hashabnah, Maaseiah,
text.DRV: 10:25 Rehum, Hasebna, Maasia,

text.NRSV: Ahiah, Hanan, Anan,
text.Rotherham: and Ahiah, Hanan, Anan;
text.KJV: 10:26 And Ahiiah, Hanan, Anan,
text.DRV: 10:26 Echaia, Hanan, Anan,

text.NRSV: Malluch, Harim, and Baanah.
text.Rotherham: Malluch, Harim, Baanah.
text.KJV: 10:27 Malluch, Harim, Baanah.
text.DRV: 10:27 Melluch, Haran, Baana:

text.NRSV: The rest of the people, the priests, the Levites, the gatekeepers, the singers, the temple servants, and all who have separated themselves from the peoples of the lands to adhere to the law of God, their wives, their sons, their daughters, all who have knowledge and understanding,
text.Rotherham: And, the rest of the people—the priests, the Levites, the doorkeepers, the singers, the Nethinim, and all who had separated themselves from the peoples of the lands unto the law of God, their wives, their sons and their daughters,—every one having knowledge and understanding,
text.KJV: 10:28 ¶ And the rest of the people, the Priests, the Leuites, the Porters, the singers, the Nethinims, and all they that had separated themselues from the people of the lands, vnto the Law of God, their wiues, their sonnes, and their daughters, euery one hauing knowledge, and hauing vnderstanding.
text.DRV: 10:28 And the rest of the people, Priests, Levites, Porters, and singing men, Nathineites, and all that separated themselves from the peoples of the lands to the law of God, their wives, their sons, and their daughters.

text.NRSV: join with their kin, their nobles, and enter into a curse and an oath to walk in God’s law, which was given by Moses the servant of God, and to observe and do all the commandments of the Lord our Lord and his ordinances and his statutes.
text.Rotherham: were holding fast unto their distinguished brethren, and were entering into a curse and into an oath, to walk in the law of God, which was given through Moses the servant of God,—and to observe and do, all the commandments of Yahweh our Lord, and his regulations, and his statutes;
text.KJV: 10:29 They claue to their brethren their nobles, and entred into a curse, and into an oath to walke in Gods law, which was giuen by Moses the seruant of God, and to obserue and doe all the commandements of the Lord our Lord, and his Iudgements, and his statutes:
text.DRV: 10:29 All that could understand, promising for their brethren, their Princes, and they that came to promise, and swear that they would walk in the law of God, which he gave in the hand of Moyses the servant of God, that they would do and keep all the commandments of the Lord our God, and his judgments and his ceremonies.

text.NRSV: We will not give our daughters to the peoples of the land or take their daughters for our sons;
text.Rotherham: and that we would not give our daughters unto the peoples of the land,—and, their daughters, would we not take for our sons;
text.KJV: 10:30 And that we would not giue our daughters vnto the people of the land, nor take their daughters for our sonnes.
text.DRV: 10:30 And that we would not give our daughters to the people of the land, and their daughters we would not take to our sons.

text.NRSV: and if the peoples of the land bring in merchandise or any grain on the sabbath day to sell, we will not buy it from them on the sabbath or on a holy day; and we will forego the crops of the seventh year and the exaction of every debt.
text.Rotherham: and, if the peoples of the land should be bringing in wares, or any corn on the sabbath day, to sell, we would not buy of them on the sabbath, or on a holy day,—and that we would remit the seventh year, and the loan of every hand.
text.KJV: 10:31 And if the people of the land bring ware or any victuals on the Sabbath day, to sell, that we would not buy it of them on the Sabbath, or on the holyday, and that wee would leaue the seuenth yeere, and the exaction of euerie debt.
text.DRV: 10:31 The peoples of the land, which bring in things to sell, & all things to be used, to sell them on the Sabbath day, we will not take it of them in the Sabbath, & in the sanctified day. And we will let pass the seventh year, & the exaction of every hand.

text.NRSV: We also lay on ourselves the obligation to charge ourselves yearly one-third of a shekel for the service of the house of our God:
text.Rotherham: And we laid on ourselves charges, appointing for ourselves the third of a shekel, yearly,—for the service of the house of our God:
text.KJV: 10:32 Also we made ordinances for vs, to charge our selues yeerely, with the third part of a shekel, for the seruice of the house of our God,
text.DRV: 10:32 And we will ordain precepts upon ourselves, to give the third part of a sicle every year to the work of the house of our God.

text.NRSV: for the rows of bread, the regular grain offering, the regular burnt offering, the sabbaths, the new moons, the appointed festivals, the sacred donations, and the sin offerings to make atonement for Israel, and for all the work of the house of our God.
text.Rotherham: for the bread to set in array, and the continual meal-offering, and for the continual ascending-sacrifice, of the sabbaths, of the new moons, for the appointed feasts, and for things hallowed, and for victims bearing sin, to put a propitiatory-covering over Israel,—and for all the work of the house of our God.
text.KJV: 10:33 For the shew-bread, and for the continuall meate-offering, and for the continuall burnt offering, of the Sabbaths, of the new moones, for the set-feastes, and for the holy things, and for the sin-offerings, to make an atonement for Israel, and for all the worke of the house of our God.
text.DRV: 10:33 to the loaves of proposition, and to the continual sacrifice, & for a continual holocaust in the Sabbaths, in the Calends, in the Solemnities, and in the sanctified, and for sin: that propitiation may be made for Israel, and unto all use of the house of our God.

text.NRSV: We have also cast lots among the priests, the Levites, and the people, for the wood offering, to bring it into the house of our God, by ancestral houses, at appointed times, year by year, to burn on the altar of the Lord our God, as it is written in the law.
text.Rotherham: Also, lots, did we cast, concerning the offering of wood among the priests, the Levites, and the people, to bring it unto the house of our God, by our ancestral houses, at times arranged, year by year,—to burn upon the altar of Yahweh our God, as it is written in the law.
text.KJV: 10:34 And we cast the lots among the priests, the Leuites, and the people, for the wood offering, to bring it into the house of our God, after the houses of our fathers, at times appointed, yeere by yeere, to burne vpon the altar of the Lord our God, as it is written in the law:
text.DRV: 10:34 We therefore did cast lots concerning the oblation of wood between the Priests, and the Levites, and the people, that it should be brought into the house of our God by the houses of our fathers at set times, from year to year: that it might burn upon the altar of the Lord our God as it is written in the law of Moyses:

text.NRSV: We obligate ourselves to bring the first fruits of our soil and the first fruits of all fruit of every tree, year by year, to the house of the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And that we would bring in the firstfruits of our ground, and the firstfruit of all fruit of all trees, year by year,—unto the house of Yahweh;
text.KJV: 10:35 And to bring the first fruits of our ground, and the first fruites of all fruit of all trees, yeere by yeere, vnto the house of the Lord.
text.DRV: 10:35 And that we would bring the first born of our land, and the first fruits of all the fruit of every tree, from year to year, in the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: also to bring to the house of our God, to the priests who minister in the house of our God, the firstborn of our sons and of our livestock, as it is written in the law, and the firstlings of our herds and of our flocks;
text.Rotherham: also that, the firstborn of our sons, and of our cattle, as it is written in the law,—and the firstlings of our herds and of our flocks, we would bring in unto the house of our God, unto the priests who should be in attendance in the house of our God;
text.KJV: 10:36 Also the first-borne of our sonnes, and of our cattell (as it is written in the lawe) and the firstlings of our heards, and of our flockes, to bring to the house of our God, vnto the priests that minister in the house of our God:
text.DRV: 10:36 and the first fruits of our sons, and of our cattle, as it is written in the law, and the first fruits of our oxen, and of our sheep, that they might be offered in the house of our God, to the Priests which minister in the house of our God.

text.NRSV: and to bring the first of our dough, and our contributions, the fruit of every tree, the wine and the oil, to the priests, to the chambers of the house of our God; and to bring to the Levites the tithes from our soil, for it is the Levites who collect the tithes in all our rural towns.
text.Rotherham: and, the first part of our meal and our heave-offerings and the fruit of all trees, new wine and oil, would we bring in unto the priests, into the chambers of the house of our God, and the tithe of our ground unto the Levites,—the Levites themselves, taking the tithes in all our cities of agriculture.
text.KJV: 10:37 And that we should bring the first fruits of our dough, and our offerings, and the fruit of all maner of trees, of wine and of oile, vnto the priests, to the chambers of the house of our God, and the tithes of our ground vnto the Leuites, that the same Leuites might haue the tithes, in all the cities of our tillage.
text.DRV: 10:37 And the first fruits of our meats, and of our libaments, & the fruits of every tree, of vintage also and of oil we will bring to the Priests, unto the treasury of our God, and the tenth part of our land to the Levites. The Levites themselves shall receive the tithes out of all the cities of our works.

text.NRSV: And the priest, the descendant of Aaron, shall be with the Levites when the Levites receive the tithes; and the Levites shall bring up a tithe of the tithes to the house of our God, to the chambers of the storehouse.
text.Rotherham: And the priest the son of Aaron should be with the Levites, when the Levites should take the tithes,—and the Levites, should bring up the tithe of the tithe, unto the house of our God, into the chambers pertaining unto the treasure-house.
text.KJV: 10:38 And the priest the sonne of Aaron, shall be with the Leuites, when the Leuites take tithes, and the Leuites shal bring vp the tithe of the tithes vnto the house of our God, to the chambers into the treasure house.
text.DRV: 10:38 And the Priest the son of Aaron shall be with the Levites in the tithes of the Levites, and the Levites shall offer the tenth part of their tithe in the house of our God, to the treasury in the treasure-house.

text.NRSV: For the people of Israel and the sons of Levi shall bring the contribution of grain, wine, and oil to the storerooms where the vessels of the sanctuary are, and where the priests that minister, and the gatekeepers and the singers are. We will not neglect the house of our God.
text.Rotherham: For, into the chambers, should the sons of Israel and the sons of Levi bring in the heave-offering of the corn, the new wine and the oil, since, there, are the utensils of the sanctuary, and the priests who are in attendance, and the doorkeepers, and the singers,—so would we not neglect the house of our God.
text.KJV: 10:39 For the children of Israel, and the children of Leui, shall bring the offering of the corne, of the new wine, and the oyle, vnto the chambers, where are the vessels of the sanctuarie, and the priests that minister, and the porters, and the singers, and we will not forsake the house of our God.
text.DRV: 10:39 For the children of Israel and the children of Levi shall carry to the treasury the first fruits of corn, of wine, and of oil: and the sanctified vessels shall be there, and the Priests, and singing men, and porters, and ministers, and we will not leave the house of our God.

text.NRSV: Now the leaders of the people lived in Jerusalem; and the rest of the people cast lots to bring one out of ten to live in the holy city Jerusalem, while nine-tenths remained in the other towns.
text.Rotherham: And the rulers of the people dwelt in Jerusalem,—and, the rest of the people, cast lots, to bring in one out of ten to dwell in Jerusalem, the holy city, and nine parts in [other] cities.
text.KJV: 11:1 And the rulers of the people dwelt at Ierusalem: the rest of the people also cast lots, to bring one of tenne, to dwell in Ierusalem, the holy citie, and nine parts to dwell in other cities.
text.DRV: 11:1 And the Princes of the people dwelt in Jerusalem: but the rest of the people cast lots, to take one part of ten that should dwell in Jerusalem the holy city, and nine parts in the cities.

text.NRSV: And the people blessed all those who willingly offered to live in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And the people bestowed a blessing on all the men, who willingly offered themselves to dwell in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 11:2 And the people blessed all the men, that willingly offered themselues, to dwell at Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 11:2 And the people blessed all the men that had voluntarily offered themselves to dwell in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: These are the leaders of the province who lived in Jerusalem; but in the towns of Judah all lived on their property in their towns: Israel, the priests, the Levites, the temple servants, and the descendants of Solomon’s servants.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the chiefs of the province, who dwelt in Jerusalem, but, in the cities of Judah, dwelt every man in his possession throughout their cities, Israel, the priests, and the Levites and the Nethinim, and the Sons of the Servants of Solomon.
text.KJV: 11:3 ¶ Now these are the chiefe of the prouince that dwelt in Ierusalem: but in the cities of Iudah dwelt euerie one in his possession in their cities, to wit, Israel, the priests, and the Leuites, and the Nethinims, and the children of Solomons seruants.
text.DRV: 11:3 These therefore are the Princes of the Province, which dwelt in Jerusalem, and in the cities of Juda. And every one dwelt in his possession, in their cities, Israel, the Priests, the Levites, the Nathineites, and the children of the servants of Salomon.

text.NRSV: And in Jerusalem lived some of the Judahites and of the Benjaminites. Of the Judahites: Athaiah son of Uzziah son of Zechariah son of Amariah son of Shephatiah son of Mahalalel, of the descendants of Perez;
text.Rotherham: And, in Jerusalem, dwelt certain of the sons of Judah and of the sons of Benjamin,—Of the sons of Judah, Athaiah son of Uzziah, son of Zechariah, son of Amariah, son of Shephatiah, son of Mahalalel, of the sons of Perez;
text.KJV: 11:4 And at Ierusalem dwelt certaine of the children of Iudah, and of the children of Beniamin. Of the children of Iudah: Athaiah the sonne of Uzziah, the sonne of Zechariah, the sonne of Amariah, the sonne of Shephatiah, the sonne of Mahalaleel, of the children of Perez.
text.DRV: 11:4 And in Jerusalem there dwelt of the children of Juda, and of the children of Benjamin: of the children of Juda, Athaias the son of Aziam, the son of Zacharias, the son of Amarias, the son of Saphatias, the son of Malaleel: of the children of Phares,

text.NRSV: and Maaseiah son of Baruch son of Col-hozeh son of Hazaiah son of Adaiah son of Joiarib son of Zechariah son of the Shilonite.
text.Rotherham: and Maaseiah son of Baruch, son of Col-hozeh, son of Hazaiah, son of Adaiah, son of Joiarib, son of Zechariah, son of the Shilonite.
text.KJV: 11:5 And Maaseiah the sonne of Baruch the sonne of Col-Hozeh, the sonne of Hazaiah the sonne of Adaiah, the sonne of Ioiarib, the sonne of Zechariah, the sonne of Shiloni.
text.DRV: 11:5 Maasia the son of Baruch, the son of Cholhoza, the son of Hazia, the son of Adaia, the son of Joiarib, the son of Zacharias, the son of a Silonite.

text.NRSV: All the descendants of Perez who lived in Jerusalem were four hundred sixty-eight valiant warriors.
text.Rotherham: All the sons of Perez who were dwelling in Jerusalem, four hundred and sixty-eight, men of ability.
text.KJV: 11:6 All the sonnes of Perez that dwelt at Ierusalem, were foure hundred threescore and eight valiant men.
text.DRV: 11:6 All these the children of Phares, which dwelt in Jerusalem, four hundred sixty eight, valiant men.

text.NRSV: And these are the Benjaminites: Sallu son of Meshullam son of Joed son of Pedaiah son of Kolaiah son of Maaseiah son of Ithiel son of Jeshaiah.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Benjamin,—Sallu son of Meshullam, son of Joed, son of Pedaiah, son of Kolaiah, son of Maaseiah, son of Ithiel, son of Jeshaiah;
text.KJV: 11:7 And these are the sonnes of Beniamin: Sallu the sonne of Meshullam, the sonne of Ioed, the sonne of Pedaiah, the sonne of Kolaiah, the sonne of Maaseiah, the sonne of Ithiel, the sonne of Iesaiah.
text.DRV: 11:7 And these are the children of Benjamin: Sellum the son of Mosollam, the son of Ioed, the son of Phadaia, the son of Colaia, the son of Masia, the son of Etheel, the son of Isaia,

text.NRSV: And his brothers Gabbai, Sallai: nine hundred twenty-eight.
text.Rotherham: and, after him, Gabbai, Sallai,—nine hundred and twenty-eight.
text.KJV: 11:8 And after him Gabai, Sallai, nine hundred twentie and eight.
text.DRV: 11:8 and after him Gebbai, Sellai, nine hundred twenty eight.

text.NRSV: Joel son of Zichri was their overseer; and Judah son of Hassenuah was second in charge of the city.
text.Rotherham: And, Joel son of Zichri, was in charge over them,—and, Judah son of Hassenuah, was over the city, as second.
text.KJV: 11:9 And Ioel the sonne of Zichri was their ouerseer: and Iudah the sonne of Senuah, was second ouer the city.
text.DRV: 11:9 and Joel the son of Zechri the overseer of them, and Judas the son of Senua second over the city.

text.NRSV: Of the priests: Jedaiah son of Joiarib, Jachin,
text.Rotherham: Of the priests, Jedaiah son of Joiarib, Jachin;
text.KJV: 11:10 Of the Priests: Iedaiah the sonne of Ioiarib, Iachin;
text.DRV: 11:10 And of the Priests, Idaia the son of Joarib, Jachin,

text.NRSV: Seraiah son of Hilkiah son of Meshullam son of Zadok son of Meraioth son of Ahitub, officer of the house of God,
text.Rotherham: Seraiah son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub, chief ruler of the house of God;
text.KJV: 11:11 Seraiah the sonne of Hilkiah, the sonne of Meshullam, the sonne of Zadok, the sonne of Meraioth, the sonne of Ahitub, was the ruler of the house of God.
text.DRV: 11:11 Saraia the son of Helcias, the son of Mosollam, the son of Sadoc, the son of Meraioth, the son of Achitob the Prince of the house of God,

text.NRSV: and their associates who did the work of the house, eight hundred twenty-two; and Adaiah son of Jeroham son of Pelaliah son of Amzi son of Zechariah son of Pashhur son of Malchijah,
text.Rotherham: and their brethren who were doing the work of the house, eight hundred and twenty-two,—and Adaiah son of Jeroham, son of Pelaliah, son of Amzi, son of Zechariah, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah;
text.KJV: 11:12 And their brethren that did the worke of the house, were eight hundred twentie and two: and Adaiah the sonne of Ieroham, the sonne of Pelaliah, the sonne of Amzi, the sonne of Zechariah, the sonne of Pashur, the sonne of Malchiah,
text.DRV: 11:12 and their brethren that do the works of the temple: eight hundred twenty two. And Adaia the son of Jeroham, the son of Phelelia, the son of Amsi, the son of Zacharias, the son of Pheshur, the son of Melchias,

text.NRSV: and his associates, heads of ancestral houses, two hundred forty-two; and Amashsai son of Azarel son of Ahzai son of Meshillemoth son of Immer,
text.Rotherham: and his brethren, ancestral chiefs, two hundred and forty-two,—and Amashsai son of Azarel, son of Ahzai, son of Meshillemoth, son of Immer;
text.KJV: 11:13 And his brethren, chiefe of the fathers, two hundred fourty and two: and Amashai the sonne of Azareel, the sonne of Ahasai, the sonne of Meshilemoth, the sonne of Immer.
text.DRV: 11:13 and his brethren the Princes of the fathers: two hundred forty two. And Amassai the son of Azreel, the son of Ahazi, the son of Mosolamoth, the son of Emmer,

text.NRSV: and their associates, valiant warriors, one hundred twenty-eight; their overseer was Zabdiel son of Haggedolim.
text.Rotherham: and their brethren, strong men of ability, a hundred and twenty-eight,—and, he who was in charge of them, was Zabdiel, son of Haggedolim.
text.KJV: 11:14 And their brethren mighty men of valour, an hundred twenty and eight; and their ouerseer was Zaddiel, the sonne of one of the great men.
text.DRV: 11:14 and their brethren exceeding mighty: an hundred twenty eight, and their overseer Zabdiel son of the mighty ones.

text.NRSV: And of the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub son of Azrikam son of Hashabiah son of Bunni;
text.Rotherham: And, of the Levites, Shemaiah son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, son of Bunni;
text.KJV: 11:15 Also of the Leuites: Shemaiah the sonne of Hashub, the sonne of Azrikam, the sonne of Hashabiah, the sonne of Bunni.
text.DRV: 11:15 And of the Levites Semeia the son of Hasub, the son of Azaricam, the son of Hasabia, the son of Boni,

text.NRSV: and Shabbethai and Jozabad, of the leaders of the Levites, who were over the outside work of the house of God;
text.Rotherham: and Shabbethai and Jozabad, over the outside business of the house of God, of the chiefs of the Levites;
text.KJV: 11:16 And Shabbethai, and Iozabad, of the chiefe of the Leuits, had the ouersight of the outward businesse of the house of God.
text.DRV: 11:16 and Sabathai and Jozabed, over all the works, that were without the house of God, of the Princes of the Levites.

text.NRSV: and Mattaniah son of Mica son of Zabdi son of Asaph, who was the leader to begin the thanksgiving in prayer, and Bakbukiah, the second among his associates; and Abda son of Shammua son of Galal son of Jeduthun.
text.Rotherham: and Mattaniah son of Mica, son of Zabdi, son of Asaph, leader of the praise who giveth thanks, in prayer, and Bakbukiah the second among his brethren,—and Obadiah son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun.
text.KJV: 11:17 And Mattaniah the sonne of Micha, the sonne of Zabdi, the sonne of Asaph, was the principall to beginne the thankesgiuing in prayer: and Bakbukiah the second among his brethren, and Abda the sonne of Shammua, the sonne of Galal, the sonne of Ieduthun.
text.DRV: 11:17 And Mathania the son of Micha, the son of Zebedei, the son of Asaph Prince to praise, and to confess in prayer, and Becbecia second of his brethren, and Abda the son of Samua, the son of Galal, the son of Idithun.

text.NRSV: All the Levites in the holy city were two hundred eighty-four.
text.Rotherham: All the Levites in the holy city, were two hundred and eighty-four.
text.KJV: 11:18 All the Leuites in the holy City, were two hundred, fourescore and foure.
text.DRV: 11:18 All the Levites in the holy city two hundred eighty four.

text.NRSV: The gatekeepers, Akkub, Talmon and their associates, who kept watch at the gates, were one hundred seventy-two.
text.Rotherham: And, the door-keepers—Akkub, Talmon, and their brethren who were keeping watch in the gates, were a hundred and seventy-two.
text.KJV: 11:19 Moreouer, the porters, Akkub, Talmon, and their brethren that kept the gates, were an hundred seuenty and two.
text.DRV: 11:19 And the porters, Accub, Telmon, and their brethren, which kept the doors: an hundred seventy two.

text.NRSV: And the rest of Israel, and of the priests and the Levites, were in all the towns of Judah, all of them in their inheritance.
text.Rotherham: But, the residue of Israel, the priests, the Levites, were in all the cities of Judah, every one in his inheritance.
text.KJV: 11:20 ¶ And the residue of Israel, of the Priests and the Leuites, were in all the cities of Iudah, euery one in his inheritance.
text.DRV: 11:20 And the rest of Israel the Priests and the Levites in all the cities of Juda, every man in his possession.

text.NRSV: But the temple servants lived on Ophel; and Ziha and Gishpa were over the temple servants.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the Nethinim, were dwelling in Ophel,—and, Ziha and Gishpa, were over the Nethinim.
text.KJV: 11:21 But the Nethinims dwelt in Ophel: and Ziha, and Gispa were ouer the Nethinims.
text.DRV: 11:21 And the Nathineites, that dwelt in Ophel, and Siaha, and Gaspha of the Nathineites.

text.NRSV: The overseer of the Levites in Jerusalem was Uzzi son of Bani son of Hashabiah son of Mattaniah son of Mica, of the descendants of Asaph, the singers, in charge of the work of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: And, the overseer of the Levites in Jerusalem, was Uzzi son of Bani, son of Hashabiah, son of Mattaniah, son of Mica,—of the sons of Asaph the singers, to take lead in the business of the house of God.
text.KJV: 11:22 The ouerseer also of the Leuites at Ierusalem, was Uzzi the sonne of Bani, the son of Hashabiah, the sonne of Mattaniah, the sonne of Micha: Of the sonnes of Asaph, the singers were ouer the businesse of the house of God.
text.DRV: 11:22 And the overseer of the Levites in Jerusalem, Azzi the son of Bani, the son of Hasabia, the son of Mathania, the son of Micha. Of the children of Asaph, the singing men in the ministry of the house of God.

text.NRSV: For there was a command from the king concerning them, and a settled provision for the singers, as was required every day.
text.Rotherham: For, the commandment of the king, was upon them,—as to a fixed provision for the singers, the need of a day upon its day.
text.KJV: 11:23 For it was the kings commandement concerning them, that a certaine portion should be for the singers, due for euery day.
text.DRV: 11:23 For the king's commandment was upon them, and an order among the singing men day by day.

text.NRSV: And Pethahiah son of Meshezabel, of the descendants of Zerah son of Judah, was at the king’s hand in all matters concerning the people.
text.Rotherham: And, Pethahiah son of Meshezabel, of the sons of Zerah, son of Judah, was at the hand of the king, in every matter pertaining to the people.
text.KJV: 11:24 And Pethahiah the sonne of Meshezabel, of the children of Zerah the sonne of Iudah, was at the kings hand in all matters concerning the people.
text.DRV: 11:24 And Phathahia the son of Mesezebel of the children of Zara the son of Juda in the hand of the king, according to every word of the people,

text.NRSV: And as for the villages, with their fields, some of the people of Judah lived in Kiriath-arba and its villages, and in Dibon and its villages, and in Jekabzeel and its villages,
text.Rotherham: And, as for the villages in their fields, some of the sons of Judah, dwelt in Kiriath-arba, and the hamlets thereof, and in Dibon, and the hamlets thereof, and in Jekabzeel, and the villages thereof;
text.KJV: 11:25 And for the villages, with their fields, some of the children of Iudah dwelt at Kiriath-arba, and in the villages thereof; and at Dibon, and in the villages thereof, and at Iekabzeel, and in the villages thereof:
text.DRV: 11:25 and in the houses through all their countries. Of the children of Juda there dwelt in Cariatharbe, and in her daughters: and in Dibon, and in her daughters, and in Cabseel, and in the villages thereof,

text.NRSV: and in Jeshua and in Moladah and Beth-pelet,
text.Rotherham: and in Jeshua, and in Moladah, and in Beth-pelet;
text.KJV: 11:26 And at Ieshua, and at Moladah, and at Beth-phelet,
text.DRV: 11:26 and in Jesue, and in Molada, and in Bethphaleth,

text.NRSV: in Hazar-shual, in Beer-sheba and its villages,
text.Rotherham: and in Hazar-shual, and in Beer-sheba, and the hamlets thereof;
text.KJV: 11:27 And at Hazer-Shual, and at Beer-sheba and in the villages thereof:
text.DRV: 11:27 and in Hasersual, and in Bersabee, and in her daughters,

text.NRSV: in Ziklag, in Meconah and its villages,
text.Rotherham: and in Ziklag, and in Meconah, and in the hamlets thereof,
text.KJV: 11:28 And at Ziglag, and at Mekonah, and in the villages thereof:
text.DRV: 11:28 and in Siceleg, and in Mochona, and in her daughters,

text.NRSV: in En-rimmon, in Zorah, in Jarmuth,
text.Rotherham: and in En-rimmmon, and in Zorah, and in Jarmuth;
text.KJV: 11:29 And at En-Rimmon, and at Zareah, and at Iarmuth,
text.DRV: 11:29 and in Remmon, and in Saraa, and in Jerimuth,

text.NRSV: Zanoah, Adullam, and their villages, Lachish and its fields, and Azekah and its villages. So they camped from Beer-sheba to the valley of Hinnom.
text.Rotherham: Zanoah, Adullam and their villages, Lachish, and the fields thereof, Azekah, and the hamlets thereof,—so they encamped from Beer-sheba, unto the valley of Hinnom.
text.KJV: 11:30 Zanoah, Adullam, and in their villages, at Lachish, and the fieldes thereof: at Azekah, and in the villages thereof. And they dwelt from Beer-sheba, vnto the valley of Hinnom.
text.DRV: 11:30 Zanoa, Odollam, and in their towns, Lachis and in her countries, Azeca, and in her daughters. And they abode in Bersabee unto the vale of Ennom.

text.NRSV: The people of Benjamin also lived from Geba onward, at Michmash, Aija, Bethel and its villages,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Benjamin, [dwelt] from Geba, to Michmas and Aija, and Bethel, and the hamlets thereof:
text.KJV: 11:31 The children also of Beniamin, from Geba, dwelt at Michmash, and Aiia, and Beth-el, and in their villages:
text.DRV: 11:31 And the children of Benjamin, of Geba, Mecmas, and Hai, and Bethel, and her daughters,

text.NRSV: Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah,
text.Rotherham: Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah;
text.KJV: 11:32 And at Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah,
text.DRV: 11:32 in Anathoth, Nob, Anania,

text.NRSV: Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim,
text.Rotherham: Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim;
text.KJV: 11:33 Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim,
text.DRV: 11:33 Asor, Rama, Gethaim,

text.NRSV: Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat,
text.Rotherham: Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat;
text.KJV: 11:34 Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat,
text.DRV: 11:34 Hadid, Seboim, and Neballat, Lod,

text.NRSV: Lod, and Ono, the valley of artisans.
text.Rotherham: Lod and Ono, the valley of craftsmen.
text.KJV: 11:35 Lod, and Ono, the valley of craftes-men.
text.DRV: 11:35 and Ono the valley of artificers.

text.NRSV: And certain divisions of the Levites in Judah were joined to Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, of the Levites, certain courses of Judah, pertained unto Benjamin.
text.KJV: 11:36 And of the Leuites, were diuisions in Iudah, and in Beniamin.
text.DRV: 11:36 And of the Levites were portions of Juda, and Benjamin.

text.NRSV: These are the priests and the Levites who came up with Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua: Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra,
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the priests and the Levites, who came up with Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua,—Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra;
text.KJV: 12:1 Now these are the Priests and the Leuits that went vp with Zerubbabel the sonne of Shealtiel, and Ieshua: Seraiah, Ieremiah, Ezra,
text.DRV: 12:1 And these are the Priests and Levites, that came up with Zorobabel the son of Salathiel, and Josue: Saraia, Jeremias, Esdras,

text.NRSV: Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,
text.Rotherham: Amariah, Malluch, Hattush;
text.KJV: 12:2 Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,
text.DRV: 12:2 Amaria, Melluch, Hattus.

text.NRSV: Shecaniah, Rehum, Meremoth,
text.Rotherham: Shecaniah, Rehum, Meremoth;
text.KJV: 12:3 Shecaniah, Rehum, Merimoth,
text.DRV: 12:3 Sebenias, Rheum, Merimuth,

text.NRSV: Iddo, Ginnethoi, Abijah,
text.Rotherham: Iddo, Ginnethoi, Abijah;
text.KJV: 12:4 Iddo, Ginnetho, Abiiah,
text.DRV: 12:4 Addo, Genthon, Abia,

text.NRSV: Mijamin, Maadiah, Bilgah,
text.Rotherham: Mijamin, Maadiah, Bilgah;
text.KJV: 12:5 Miamin, Madish, Bilgah,
text.DRV: 12:5 Miamin, Madia, Belga,

text.NRSV: Shemaiah, Joiarib, Jedaiah,
text.Rotherham: Shemaiah, and Joiarib, Jedaiah;
text.KJV: 12:6 Shemaiah, & Ioiarib, Iedaiah,
text.DRV: 12:6 Semeia, and Joiarib, Idaia, Sellum, Amoc, Helcias,

text.NRSV: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah. These were the leaders of the priests and of their associates in the days of Jeshua.
text.Rotherham: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah,—these, were the chiefs of the priests and their brethren, in the days of Jeshua.
text.KJV: 12:7 Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Iedaiah: these were the chiefe of the Priests, and of their brethren in the dayes of Ieshua.
text.DRV: 12:7 Idaia. These are the Princes of the Priests, & their brethren in the days of Josue.

text.NRSV: And the Levites: Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, and Mattaniah, who with his associates was in charge of the songs of thanksgiving.
text.Rotherham: And, the Levites, Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, Mattaniah,—over the choirs, he and his brethren;
text.KJV: 12:8 Moreouer the Leuites: Ieshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Iudah, and Mattaniah, which was ouer the thankesgiuing, he and his brethren.
text.DRV: 12:8 Moreover the Levites, Jesua, Bennui, Cedmihel, Sarebia, Juda, Mathanias, over the hymns they & their brethren:

text.NRSV: And Bakbukiah and Unno their associates stood opposite them in the service.
text.Rotherham: and Bakbukiah and Unno their brethren, were over against them, in wards.
text.KJV: 12:9 Also Bakbukiah, and Unni; their brethren, were ouer against them in the watches.
text.DRV: 12:9 And Becbecia, and Hannia and their brethren every one in his office.

text.NRSV: Jeshua was the father of Joiakim, Joiakim the father of Eliashib, Eliashib the father of Joiada,
text.Rotherham: And Jeshua, begat Joiakim,—and Joiakim, begat Eliashib, and, Eliashib, [begat] Joiada;
text.KJV: 12:10 ¶ And Ieshua begate Ioiakim, Ioiakim also begate Eliashib, and Eliashib begate Ioiada,
text.DRV: 12:10 And Josue begat Ioacim, and Ioacim begat Eliasib, and Eliasib begat Joiada,

text.NRSV: Joiada the father of Jonathan, and Jonathan the father of Jaddua.
text.Rotherham: and, Joiada, begat Jonathan,—and, Jonathan, begat Jaddua.
text.KJV: 12:11 And Ioiada begate Ionathan, and Ionathan begate Iaddua.
text.DRV: 12:11 and Joiada begat Jonathan, and Jonathan begat Ieddoa.

text.NRSV: In the days of Joiakim the priests, heads of ancestral houses, were: of Seraiah, Meraiah; of Jeremiah, Hananiah;
text.Rotherham: And, in the days of Joiakim, were priests, ancestral chiefs,—of Seraiah, Meraiah, of Jeremiah, Hananiah;
text.KJV: 12:12 And in the dayes of Ioiakim, were Priests the chiefe of the fathers: of Seraiah, Meraiah: of Ieremiah, Hananiah:
text.DRV: 12:12 And in the days of Ioacim the Priests and Princes of the families were: Of Saraia, Maraia: Of Jeremias, Hanania:

text.NRSV: of Ezra, Meshullam; of Amariah, Jehohanan;
text.Rotherham: of Ezra, Meshullam,—of Amariah, Jehohanan;
text.KJV: 12:13 Of Ezra, Meshullam: of Amariah, Iehohanan:
text.DRV: 12:13 Of Esdras, Mosollam: Of Amaria, Johanan:

text.NRSV: of Malluchi, Jonathan; of Shebaniah, Joseph;
text.Rotherham: of Malluchi, Jonathan,—of Shebaniah, Joseph;
text.KJV: 12:14 Of Melicu, Ionathan: of Shebaniah, Ioseph:
text.DRV: 12:14 Of Milicho, Jonathan: Of Sebenia, Joseph:

text.NRSV: of Harim, Adna; of Meraioth, Helkai;
text.Rotherham: of Harim, Adna,—of Meraioth, Helkai;
text.KJV: 12:15 Of Harim, Adna: of Meraioth, Helkai:
text.DRV: 12:15 Of Haram, Edna: Of Maraioth, Helci:

text.NRSV: of Iddo, Zechariah; of Ginnethon, Meshullam;
text.Rotherham: of Iddo, Zechariah,—of Ginnethon, Meshullam;
text.KJV: 12:16 Of Iodo, Zechariah: of Ginnethon, Meshullam:
text.DRV: 12:16 Of Adaia, Zacharia: Of Genthon, Mosollam:

text.NRSV: of Abijah, Zichri; of Miniamin, of Moadiah, Piltai;
text.Rotherham: of Abijah, Zichri,—of Miniamin, of Moadiah, Piltai;
text.KJV: 12:17 Of Abijah, Zichri: of Miniamin, of Moadiah, Piltai:
text.DRV: 12:17 Of Abia, Zechri: Of Miamin and Moadia, Phelti:

text.NRSV: of Bilgah, Shammua; of Shemaiah, Jehonathan;
text.Rotherham: of Bilgah, Shammua,—of Shemaiah, Jehonathan;
text.KJV: 12:18 Of Bilgah, Shammua: of Shemaiah, Iehonathan:
text.DRV: 12:18 Of Belga, Sammua: Of Semaia, Jonathan:

text.NRSV: of Joiarib, Mattenai; of Jedaiah, Uzzi;
text.Rotherham: and, of Joiarib, Mattenai,—of Jedaiah, Uzzi;
text.KJV: 12:19 And of Ioiarib, Mattenai: of Iedaiah, Uzzi:
text.DRV: 12:19 Of Joiarib, Mathanai: Of Iodaia, Azzi:

text.NRSV: of Sallai, Kallai; of Amok, Eber;
text.Rotherham: of Sallai, Kallai,—of Amok, Eber;
text.KJV: 12:20 Of Sallai, Kallai: of Amok, Eber:
text.DRV: 12:20 Of Sellai, Celai: Of Amoc, Heber:

text.NRSV: of Hilkiah, Hashabiah; of Jedaiah, Nethanel.
text.Rotherham: of Hilkiah, Hashabiah,—of Jedaiah, Nethanel.
text.KJV: 12:21 Of Hilkiah, Hashabiah: of Iedaiah, Nethanael.
text.DRV: 12:21 Of Helcias, Hasebia: Of Idaia, Nathanael.

text.NRSV: As for the Levites, in the days of Eliashib, Joiada, Johanan, and Jaddua, there were recorded the heads of ancestral houses; also the priests until the reign of Darius the Persian.
text.Rotherham: The Levites—in the days of Eliashib, Joiada, and Johanan, and Jaddua, were recorded as ancestral chiefs,—also the priests, unto the reign of Darius the Persian.
text.KJV: 12:22 ¶ The Leuites in the dayes of Eliashib, Ioiada, and Iohanan, and Iaddua, were recorded chiefe of the fathers: also the Priests, to the reigne of Darius the Persian.
text.DRV: 12:22 The Levites in the days of Eliasib, and Joiada, and Johanan, and Ieddoa, written Princes of the families, and the Priests in the reign of Darius the Persian.

text.NRSV: The Levites, heads of ancestral houses, were recorded in the Book of the Annals until the days of Johanan son of Eliashib.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Levi, ancestral heads, were written in the book of Chronicles,—even until the days of Johanan, son of Eliashib.
text.KJV: 12:23 The sonnes of Leui, the chiefe of the fathers, were written in the booke of the Chronicles, euen vntill the dayes of Iohanan the sonne of Eliashib.
text.DRV: 12:23 The children of Levi, Princes of the families, written in the book of Chronicles of days, and unto the days of Jonathan the son of Eliasib.

text.NRSV: And the leaders of the Levites: Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua son of Kadmiel, with their associates over against them, to praise and to give thanks, according to the commandment of David the man of God, section opposite to section.
text.Rotherham: And, the chiefs of the Levites, Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua son of Kadmiel, with their brethren over against them, to praise—to give thanks, by the commandment of David the man of God,—ward joined to ward.
text.KJV: 12:24 And the chiefe of the Leuites: Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Ieshua the sonne of Kadmiel, with their brethren ouer against them, to praise and to giue thankes, according to the commandement of Dauid the man of God, ward ouer against ward.
text.DRV: 12:24 And the Princes of the Levites, Hasebia, Serebia, and Josue the son of Cedmihel: and their brethren by their courses, to praise and confess according to the precept of David the man of God, and to wait equally in order.

text.NRSV: Mattaniah, Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, and Akkub were gatekeepers standing guard at the storehouses of the gates.
text.Rotherham: Mattaniah and Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, Akkub, were watchers, doorkeepers of the ward, in the storehouses of the gates.
text.KJV: 12:25 Mattaniah, and Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, Akkub, were porters keeping the ward, at the thresholds of the gates.
text.DRV: 12:25 Mathania, and Becbecia, Obedia, and Mosollam, Telmon, Accub, keepers of the gates and of the entrances before the gates.

text.NRSV: These were in the days of Joiakim son of Jeshua son of Jozadak, and in the days of the governor Nehemiah and of the priest Ezra, the scribe.
text.Rotherham: These, were in the days of Joiakim, son of Jeshua, son of Jozadak,—and in the days of Nehemiah the pasha, and Ezra the priest the scribe.
text.KJV: 12:26 These were in the dayes of Ioiakim, the sonne of Ieshua, the sonne of Iozadak, and in the dayes of Nehemiah the gouernour, and of Ezra the Priest, the Scribe.
text.DRV: 12:26 These were in the days of Ioacim the son of Josue, the son Josedec, and in the days of Nehemias the duke, and of Esdras the Priest, and Scribe.

text.NRSV: Now at the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem they sought out the Levites in all their places, to bring them to Jerusalem to celebrate the dedication with rejoicing, with thanksgivings and with singing, with cymbals, harps, and lyres.
text.Rotherham: And, at the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem, they sought the Levites, out of all their places, to bring them to Jerusalem,—to keep the dedication and the rejoicing, both with thanksgiving and with music, cymbals, harps, and with lyres.
text.KJV: 12:27 ¶ And at the dedication of the wall of Ierusalem, they sought the Leuites out of all their places, to bring them to Ierusalem, to keepe the dedication with gladnesse, both with thankesgiuings and with singing, with cymbals, psalteries, and with harpes.
text.DRV: 12:27 And in the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem they sought Levites out of all other places, to bring them into Jerusalem, and to make the dedication and joy in giving of thanks, and song, and in cymbals, psalteries and harps.

text.NRSV: The companies of the singers gathered together from the circuit around Jerusalem and from the villages of the Netophathites;
text.Rotherham: So the sons of the singers gathered themselves together,—both out of the circuit round about Jerusalem, and out of the villages of the Netophathites;
text.KJV: 12:28 And the sonnes of the Singers gathered themselues together, both out of the plaine countrey round about Ierusalem, and from the villages of Netophathi.
text.DRV: 12:28 And the children of the singing men were gathered together out of the champion about Jerusalem, and out of the towns Nethuphati,

text.NRSV: also from Beth-gilgal and from the region of Geba and Azmaveth; for the singers had built for themselves villages around Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: also out of Beth-gilgal, and out of the fields of Geba, and Azmaveth,—for, villages, had the singers builded for themselves, round about Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 12:29 Also from the house of Gilgal, and out of the fields of Geba, and Azmaueth: for the Singers had builded them villages round about Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 12:29 and from the house of Galgal, and from the countries of Geba and Azmaveth: because the singing men did build themselves villages round about Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: And the priests and the Levites purified themselves; and they purified the people and the gates and the wall.
text.Rotherham: And the priests and the Levites purified themselves,—and purified the people, and the gates, and the wall.
text.KJV: 12:30 And the Priests and the Leuites purified themselues, and purified the people, and the gates, and the wall.
text.DRV: 12:30 And the Priests and Levites were cleansed, and they cleansed the people, and the gates, & the wall.

text.NRSV: Then I brought the leaders of Judah up onto the wall, and appointed two great companies that gave thanks and went in procession. One went to the right on the wall to the Dung Gate;
text.Rotherham: Then brought I up the rulers of Judah upon the wall,—and I appointed two large choirs, even to go in procession to the right, upon the wall, towards the dung-gate;
text.KJV: 12:31 Then I brought vp the princes of Iudah vpon the wall, and appointed two great companies of them that gaue thankes, whereof one went on the right hand vpon the wall toward the dounggate:
text.DRV: 12:31 And I made the Princes of Juda go up upon the wall, and I set two great choirs of them that should praise. And they went on the right hand upon the wall to the gate of the dunghill.

text.NRSV: and after them went Hoshaiah and half the officials of Judah,
text.Rotherham: and after them went Hoshaiah, and half the rulers of Judah;
text.KJV: 12:32 And after them went Hoshaiah, and halfe of the Princes of Iudah,
text.DRV: 12:32 And after them went Osaias, & the half part of the Princes of Juda,

text.NRSV: and Azariah, Ezra, Meshullam,
text.Rotherham: then Azariah, Ezra, and Meshullam;
text.KJV: 12:33 And Azariah, Ezra, and Meshullam,
text.DRV: 12:33 and Azarias, Esdras, & Mosollam, Judas, & Benjamin, & Semeia, & Jeremias.

text.NRSV: Judah, Benjamin, Shemaiah, and Jeremiah,
text.Rotherham: Judah, and Benjamin, and Shemaiah, and Jeremiah;
text.KJV: 12:34 Iudah, and Beniamin, and Shemaiah, and Ieremiah,
text.DRV: 12:34 And of the children of the Priests with trumpets, Zacharias the son of Jonathan, the son of Semeia, the son of Mathania, the son of Michaia, the son Zechur, the son Asaph,

text.NRSV: and some of the young priests with trumpets: Zechariah son of Jonathan son of Shemaiah son of Mattaniah son of Micaiah son of Zaccur son of Asaph;
text.Rotherham: and, of the sons of the priests, with trumpets, Zechariah—son of Jonathan—son of Shemaiah—son of Mattaniah, son of Micaiah, son of Zaccur, son of Asaph;
text.KJV: 12:35 And certaine of the Priests sonnes with trumpets: namely, Zechariah the sonne of Ionathan, the sonne of Shemaiah, the sonne of Mattaniah, the sonne of Michaiah, the sonne of Zaccur, the sonne of Asaph:
text.DRV: 12:35 and his brethren Semeia, and Azareel, Malalai, Galalai, Maai, Nathanael, and Judas, and Hanani, with the instruments of the song of David the man of God: and Esdras the scribe before them in the gate of the fountain.

text.NRSV: and his kindred, Shemaiah, Azarel, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethanel, Judah, and Hanani, with the musical instruments of David the man of God; and the scribe Ezra went in front of them.
text.Rotherham: and his brethren—Shemaiah and Azarel, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethanel, and Judah, Hanani, with the instruments of music of David, the man of God,—with Ezra the scribe before them;
text.KJV: 12:36 And his brethren, Shemaiah, and Asarael, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethanael, and Iudah, Hanani, with the musicall instruments of Dauid the man of God; and Ezra the Scribe before them.
text.DRV: 12:36 And against them there went up in the stairs of the city of David, in the rising of the wall above the house of David, and unto the gate of waters toward the East.

text.NRSV: At the Fountain Gate, in front of them, they went straight up by the stairs of the city of David, at the ascent of the wall, above the house of David, to the Water Gate on the east.
text.Rotherham: and, over they fountain gate and straight before them, they went up by the stairs of the city of David, at the going up of the wall,—above the house of David, even as far as the water-gate, eastward.
text.KJV: 12:37 And at the fountaine-gate, which was ouer against them, they went vp by the staires of the citie of Dauid, at the going vp of the wall, aboue the house of Dauid, euen vnto the water-gate, Eastward.
text.DRV: 12:37 And the second choir of thanks-givers went on the contrary side, and I after it, & the half part of the people upon the wall, & above the tower of the furnaces, and unto the broadest wall,

text.NRSV: The other company of those who gave thanks went to the left, and I followed them with half of the people on the wall, above the Tower of the Ovens, to the Broad Wall,
text.Rotherham: And, the second choir, was going over against them, I, following it,—with the half of the people upon the wall, above the tower of the ovens, even as far as the broad wall;
text.KJV: 12:38 And the other company of them that gaue thankes, went ouer against them, and I after them, and the halfe of the people vpon the wall, from beyond the towre of the fornaces, euen vnto the broad wall,
text.DRV: 12:38 and above the gate of Ephraim, and above the old gate, and above the gate of fishes and the tower of Hananeel, and the tower of Emath, and unto the gate of the flock: and they stood in the gate of the watch,

text.NRSV: and above the Gate of Ephraim, and by the Old Gate, and by the Fish Gate and the Tower of Hananel and the Tower of the Hundred, to the Sheep Gate; and they came to a halt at the Gate of the Guard.
text.Rotherham: and above the gate of Ephraim, and upon the old gate, and upon the fish-gate, and the tower of Hananel, and the tower of Hammeah, even as far as the sheep-gate,—and they came to a stand, at the gate of the guard.
text.KJV: 12:39 And from aboue the gate of Ephraim, and aboue the olde gate, and aboue the fish-gate, and the towre of Hananeel, and the towre of Meah, euen vnto the sheepegate; and they stood still in the prison gate.
text.DRV: 12:39 and there stood two choirs of them that praised in the house of God, and I, and the half part of the magistrates with me.

text.NRSV: So both companies of those who gave thanks stood in the house of God, and I and half of the officials with me;
text.Rotherham: So the two choirs, came to a stand, at the house of God,—and I, and half the deputies with me;
text.KJV: 12:40 So stood the two companies of them that gaue thankes in the house of God, and I, and the halfe of the rulers with me:
text.DRV: 12:40 And the Priests, Eliachim, Maasia, Miamin, Michea, Elioenai, Zacharia, Hanania with trumpets,

text.NRSV: and the priests Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Micaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, and Hananiah, with trumpets;
text.Rotherham: and, the priests—Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Micaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, and Hananiah, with trumpets;
text.KJV: 12:41 And the Priests: Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Michaiah, Elioenai, Zachariah, and Hananiah with trumpets:
text.DRV: 12:41 and Maasia, & Semeia, & Eleazar, & Azzi, & Johanan, and Melchia, and Ælam, and Ezer. And the singing men sang aloud, and Iezraia the overseer:

text.NRSV: and Maaseiah, Shemaiah, Eleazar, Uzzi, Jehohanan, Malchijah, Elam, and Ezer. And the singers sang with Jezrahiah as their leader.
text.Rotherham: and Maaseiah and Shemaiah and Eleazar and Uzzi and Jehohanan and Malchijah and Elam and Ezer,—and the musicians sounded, aloud with Jezrahiah who was over them.
text.KJV: 12:42 And Maaseiah, and Shemaiah, and Eleazar, and Uzzi, and Iehohanan, and Malchiiah, and Elam, and Ezer. And the Singers sang loud, with Iezrahiah their ouerseer.
text.DRV: 12:42 and they immolated in that day great victims, and rejoiced: for God had made them joyful with great joy: yea their wives also & children rejoiced, & the joy of Jerusalem was heard far off.

text.NRSV: They offered great sacrifices that day and rejoiced, for God had made them rejoice with great joy; the women and children also rejoiced. The joy of Jerusalem was heard far away.
text.Rotherham: And they sacrificed, on that day, great sacrifices, and rejoiced, for, God, had caused them to rejoice with great joy, moreover also, the women and children, rejoiced,—so that the rejoicing of Jerusalem was heard afar off.
text.KJV: 12:43 Also that day they offered great sacrifices, and reioyced; for God had made them reioyce with great ioy: the wiues also and the children reioyced: so that the ioy of Ierusalem was heard euen afarre off.
text.DRV: 12:43 They numbered also in that day men over the storehouses of the treasure, for the libaments, and for the first fruits, & for the tithes, that the Princes of the city might bring in by them in the honour of thanks-giving, Priests and Levites: because Juda was made joyful in the Priests and Levites standing by.

text.NRSV: On that day men were appointed over the chambers for the stores, the contributions, the first fruits, and the tithes, to gather into them the portions required by the law for the priests and for the Levites from the fields belonging to the towns; for Judah rejoiced over the priests and the Levites who ministered.
text.Rotherham: And there were set in charge, on that day, certain men, over the chambers for the treasures, for the heave-offerings, for the firstfruits, and for the tithes, to gather into them, out of the fields of the cities, the portions appointed by the law, for the priests, and for the Levites,—for, the joy of Judah, was over the priests and over the Levites, who were remaining.
text.KJV: 12:44 ¶ And at that time were some appointed ouer the chambers for the treasures, for the offerings, for the first fruits, and for the tithes, to gather into them out of the fields of the cities the portions of the law for the priests and Leuites: for Iudah reioyced for the Priests, & for the Leuites that waited.
text.DRV: 12:44 And they kept the watch of their God, and the observance of expiation, and the singing men, and the porters, according to the precept of David, and of Salomon his son,

text.NRSV: They performed the service of their God and the service of purification, as did the singers and the gatekeepers, according to the command of David and his son Solomon.
text.Rotherham: So they kept the charge of their God, and the charge of the purification, and [so did] the singers and the doorkeepers,—according to the commandment of David and of Solomon his son.
text.KJV: 12:45 And both the singers and the porters kept the ward of their God, and the ward of the purification, according to the commandement of Dauid, and of Solomon his sonne.
text.DRV: 12:45 because in the days of David and Asaph from the beginning there were Princes appointed of the singing men in song praising, and confessing to God.

text.NRSV: For in the days of David and Asaph long ago there was a leader of the singers, and there were songs of praise and thanksgiving to God.
text.Rotherham: For, in the days of David and Asaph, of old, there were chiefs of the singers, and songs of praise and thanksgiving, unto God.
text.KJV: 12:46 For in the dayes of Dauid and Asaph of old, there were chiefe of the singers, and songs of praise and thanksgiuing vnto God.
text.DRV: 12:46 And all Israel, in the days of Zorobabel, and in the days of Nehemias gave portions to the singing men, and to the porters day by day, and they sanctified the Levites, and the Levites sanctified the children of Aaron.

text.NRSV: In the days of Zerubbabel and in the days of Nehemiah all Israel gave the daily portions for the singers and the gatekeepers. They set apart that which was for the Levites; and the Levites set apart that which was for the descendants of Aaron.
text.Rotherham: Now, all Israel—in the days of Zerubbabel, and in the days of Nehemiah, used to give the portions of the singers and the doorkeepers, the need of a day upon its day,—and they hallowed them unto the Levites, and, the Levites, hallowed them unto the sons of Aaron.
text.KJV: 12:47 And all Israel in the dayes of Zerubbabel, and in the dayes of Nehemiah, gaue the portions of the singers, and the porters, euery day his portion, and they sanctified holy things vnto the Leuites, and the Leuites sanctified them vnto the children of Aaron.

text.NRSV: On that day they read from the book of Moses in the hearing of the people; and in it was found written that no Ammonite or Moabite should ever enter the assembly of God,
text.Rotherham: On that day, a portion, was read in the book of Moses, in the ears of the people,—and it was found written therein, that the Ammonite and the Moabite should not enter into the convocation of God, unto times age-abiding;
text.KJV: 13:1 On that day they read in the booke of Moses in the audience of the people, and therein was found written, that the Ammonite and the Moabite should not come into the Congregation of God for euer,
text.DRV: 13:1 And in that day there was read in the volume of Moyses the people hearing it: and there was found written in it, that the Ammonites and the Moabites should not enter into the Church of God for ever:

text.NRSV: because they did not meet the Israelites with bread and water, but hired Balaam against them to curse them—yet our God turned the curse into a blessing.
text.Rotherham: because they met not the sons of Israel, with bread and with water,—but hired against them Balaam, to curse them, although our God turned the curse into a blessing.
text.KJV: 13:2 Because they met not the children of Israel with bread, and with water, but hired Balaam against them, that he should curse them: howbeit our God turned the curse into a blessing.
text.DRV: 13:2 for that they met not the children of Israel with bread and water: and they hired against them Balaam to curse them: and our God turned the cursing into blessing.

text.NRSV: When the people heard the law, they separated from Israel all those of foreign descent.
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass, when they had heard the law,—that they separated all the mixed multitude from Israel.
text.KJV: 13:3 Now it came to passe when they had heard the law, that they separated from Israel all the mixed multitude.
text.DRV: 13:3 And it came to pass, when they had heard the law, they separated every stranger from Israel.

text.NRSV: Now before this, the priest Eliashib, who was appointed over the chambers of the house of our God, and who was related to Tobiah,
text.Rotherham: Now, before this, Eliashib the priest, who was set over a chamber of the house of God, was allied unto Tobiah;
text.KJV: 13:4 ¶ And before this Eliashib the priest hauing the ouersight of the chamber of the house of our God, was allied vnto Tobiah:
text.DRV: 13:4 And over this thing was Eliasib the Priest, who had been made overseer in the treasury of the house of our God, and near a-kin to Tobias.

text.NRSV: prepared for Tobiah a large room where they had previously put the grain offering, the frankincense, the vessels, and the tithes of grain, wine, and oil, which were given by commandment to the Levites, singers, and gatekeepers, and the contributions for the priests.
text.Rotherham: so he prepared him a large chamber, where aforetime they used to lay the meal-offering, the frankincense, and the utensils, and the tithe of the corn, the new wine and the oil, in charge of the Levites, and the singers, and the doorkeepers,—and the heave-offerings for the priests.
text.KJV: 13:5 And hee had prepared for him a great chamber, where aforetime they laid the meat offrings, the frankincense and the vessels, and the tithes of the corne, the new wine, and the oile, which was commanded to be giuen to the Leuites, and the singers, and the porters, and the offerings of the priests.
text.DRV: 13:5 He therefore made to himself a great treasury, and they were there before him laying up gifts, and frankincense, and vessels, and the tithe of corn, of wine, and of oil, the portions of the Levites, and of the singing men, and of the porters, and the first fruits of the Priests.

text.NRSV: While this was taking place I was not in Jerusalem, for in the thirty-second year of King Artaxerxes of Babylon I went to the king. After some time I asked leave of the king
text.Rotherham: But, throughout all this [time], was I not in Jerusalem,—for, in the thirty-second year of Artaxerxes king of Babylon, I came unto the king, and, at the end of certain days, obtained I leave of the king;
text.KJV: 13:6 But in all this time was not I at Ierusalem: for in the two and thirtieth yeere of Artaxerxes king of Babylon, came I vnto the king, and after certaine dayes, obtained I leaue of the King:
text.DRV: 13:6 But in all these things I was not in Jerusalem, because in the two and thirtieth year of Artaxerxes the king of Babylon I came to the king, and in the end of certain days I desired the king.

text.NRSV: and returned to Jerusalem. I then discovered the wrong that Eliashib had done on behalf of Tobiah, preparing a room for him in the courts of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: and came to Jerusalem,—and had intelligence of the wickedness which Eliashib had committed for Tobiah, in preparing for him a chamber, in the courts of the house of God;
text.KJV: 13:7 And I came to Ierusalem, and vnderstood of the euil that Eliashib did for Tobiah, in preparing him a chamber in the courts of the house of God.
text.DRV: 13:7 And I came to Jerusalem, and I understood the evil that Eliasib had done to Tobias, to make him a treasure in the entrances of the house of God.

text.NRSV: And I was very angry, and I threw all the household furniture of Tobiah out of the room.
text.Rotherham: and it grieved me exceedingly,—and I cast forth all the household utensils of Tobiah, outside of the chamber.
text.KJV: 13:8 And it grieued me sore, therefore I cast foorth all the houshold stuffe of Tobiah out of the chamber:
text.DRV: 13:8 And it seemed to me exceeding evil. And I threw forth the vessels of the house of Tobias out of the treasury:

text.NRSV: Then I gave orders and they cleansed the chambers, and I brought back the vessels of the house of God, with the grain offering and the frankincense.
text.Rotherham: Then commanded I, and they purified the chambers,—and I put back there, the utensils of the house of God, the meal-offering and the frankincense.
text.KJV: 13:9 Then I commanded, and they cleansed the chambers, and thither brought I againe the vessels of the house of God, with the meate offering, and the frankincense.
text.DRV: 13:9 and I commanded and they cleansed the treasury: and I brought thither again the vessels of the house of God, the sacrifice, and the frankincense.

text.NRSV: I also found out that the portions of the Levites had not been given to them; so that the Levites and the singers, who had conducted the service, had gone back to their fields.
text.Rotherham: Then came I to know, that, the portions of the Levites, had not been given [them],—so that the Levites and the singers, who had been doing the work, had fled every one to his field.
text.KJV: 13:10 ¶ And I perceiued that the portions of the Leuites had not beene giuen them: for the Leuites and the singers that did the worke, were fled euery one to his field.
text.DRV: 13:10 And I knew that the portions of the Levites had not been given: and that every man was fled into his country of the Levites, and the singing men, and of them that ministered:

text.NRSV: So I remonstrated with the officials and said, “Why is the house of God forsaken?” And I gathered them together and set them in their stations.
text.Rotherham: Therefore contended I with the deputies, and said, Wherefore is the house of God, forsaken? So I gathered them together, and set them in their place.
text.KJV: 13:11 Then contended I with the rulers, and said, Why is the house of God forsaken? And I gathered them together, and set them in their place.
text.DRV: 13:11 and I pleaded the matter against the magistrates, and said: Why have we forsaken the house of God? And I assembled them, and I made them to stand in their standings.

text.NRSV: Then all Judah brought the tithe of the grain, wine, and oil into the storehouses.
text.Rotherham: Then, all Judah, brought in the tithe of corn, and new wine, and oil, unto the treasuries;
text.KJV: 13:12 Then brought all Iudah the tithe of the corne, and the new wine, and the oyle, vnto the treasuries.
text.DRV: 13:12 And all Juda carried the tithe of the corn, wine, and oil into the store-houses:

text.NRSV: And I appointed as treasurers over the storehouses the priest Shelemiah, the scribe Zadok, and Pedaiah of the Levites, and as their assistant Hanan son of Zaccur son of Mattaniah, for they were considered faithful; and their duty was to distribute to their associates.
text.Rotherham: and I made treasurers over the treasuries—Shelemiah the priest, and Zadok the scribe, and Pedaiah from among the Levites, and, next unto them, Hanan son of Zaccur, son of Mattaniah,—for faithful were they accounted, and it was [laid] upon them to distribute unto their brethren.
text.KJV: 13:13 And I made treasurers ouer the treasuries, Shelemiah the priest, and Zadok the scribe, and of the Leuites, Pedaiah: and next to them was Hanan the sonne of Zaccur, the sonne of Mattaniah: for they were counted faithfull, and their office was to distribute vnto their brethren.
text.DRV: 13:13 And we appointed over the store-houses Selemias Priest, and Sadoc scribe, and Phadaia of the Levites, and next to them Hanan the son of Zachur, the son of Mathania: because they were approved faithful, and to them were committed the portions of the brethren.

text.NRSV: Remember me, O my God, concerning this, and do not wipe out my good deeds that I have done for the house of my God and for his service.
text.Rotherham: Remember me, O my God, concerning this,—and do not wipe out my lovingkindnesses, which I have done for the house of my God, and for those keeping charge thereof.
text.KJV: 13:14 Remember me, O my God, concerning this, and wipe not out my good deeds, that I haue done for the house of my God, and for the offices thereof.
text.DRV: 13:14 Remember me my God for this thing, and wipe not out my mercies, which I have done in the house of my God, and in his ceremonies.

text.NRSV: In those days I saw in Judah people treading wine presses on the sabbath, and bringing in heaps of grain and loading them on donkeys; and also wine, grapes, figs, and all kinds of burdens, which they brought into Jerusalem on the sabbath day; and I warned them at that time against selling food.
text.Rotherham: In those days, saw I in Judah—some treading winepresses on the sabbath, and bringing in sheaves and lading asses, moreover also wine, grapes, and figs, and every kind of burden, which they were bringing into Jerusalem on the sabbath day,—so I protested against it, as a day for them to sell provisions.
text.KJV: 13:15 ¶ In those dayes sawe I in Iudah, some treading wine presses on the Sabbath, and bringing in sheaues, and lading asses, as also wine, grapes, and figs, and all maner of burdens, which they brought into Ierusalem on the Sabbath day: and I testified against them in the day wherein they solde victuals.
text.DRV: 13:15 In those days I saw them in Juda treading the presses on the Sabbath, carrying heaps, and loading upon asses wine, and grapes, and figs, and all manner of burden, and bringing it into Jerusalem on the Sabbath day. And I charged them, that they should sell on a day that it was lawful to sell in.

text.NRSV: Tyrians also, who lived in the city, brought in fish and all kinds of merchandise and sold them on the sabbath to the people of Judah, and in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, men of Tyre, dwelt therein, who were bringing in fish—and every kind of ware for sale,—and were selling, on the sabbath, to the sons of Judah, and in Jerusalem;
text.KJV: 13:16 There dwelt men of Tyre also therein, which brought fish and all maner of ware, and solde on the Sabbath vnto the children of Iudah, and in Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 13:16 And the Tyrians dwelt in it, bringing fishes, and all things to sell: and they sold on the Sabbaths to the children of Juda in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Then I remonstrated with the nobles of Judah and said to them, “What is this evil thing that you are doing, profaning the sabbath day?
text.Rotherham: so I contended with the nobles of Judah,—and said unto them, What is this wicked thing which, ye, are doing, and profaning the sabbath day?
text.KJV: 13:17 Then I contended with the Nobles of Iudah, and sayd vnto them, What euill thing is this that ye doe, and profane the Sabbath day?
text.DRV: 13:17 And I rebuked the Princes of Juda, and said to them: What is this evil thing, that you do, and profane the day of the Sabbath?

text.NRSV: Did not your ancestors act in this way, and did not our God bring all this disaster on us and on this city? Yet you bring more wrath on Israel by profaning the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: Was it not, thus, your fathers did, and our God brought upon us all this calamity, and upon this city?—and, ye, would add indignation against Israel, by profaning the sabbath!
text.KJV: 13:18 Did not your fathers thus, and did not our God bring all this euill vpon vs, and vpon this citie? yet ye bring more wrath vpon Israel, by profaning the Sabbath.
text.DRV: 13:18 Why, did not our fathers these things, and our God brought upon us all this evil, and upon this city? And you add wrath upon Israel in violating the Sabbath.

text.NRSV: When it began to be dark at the gates of Jerusalem before the sabbath, I commanded that the doors should be shut and gave orders that they should not be opened until after the sabbath. And I set some of my servants over the gates, to prevent any burden from being brought in on the sabbath day.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the gates of Jerusalem made a shadow before the sabbath, then gave I word, and they shut the doors, and I gave word, that they should not open them, until after the sabbath,—and, some of my young men, set I near the gates, so that no burden should be brought in, on the sabbath day.
text.KJV: 13:19 And it came to passe, that when the gates of Ierusalem beganne to be darke before the Sabbath, I commanded that the gates should be shut, and charged that they should not be opened till after the Sabbath: and some of my seruants set I at the gates, that there should no burden be brought in on the Sabbath day.
text.DRV: 13:19 And it came to pass, when the gates of Jerusalem had rested on the Sabbath day, I spake: and they shut the gates, and I commanded them that they should not open them till after the Sabbath: and of my servants I appointed over the gates, that none should bring in burdens in the Sabbath day.

text.NRSV: Then the merchants and sellers of all kinds of merchandise spent the night outside Jerusalem once or twice.
text.Rotherham: But the traders and sellers of all kinds of wares lodged outside Jerusalem once or twice.
text.KJV: 13:20 So the merchants, and sellers of all kinde of ware, lodged without Ierusalem once or twice.
text.DRV: 13:20 And the merchants, and they that sold all merchandise, tarried without Jerusalem once and again.

text.NRSV: But I warned them and said to them, “Why do you spend the night in front of the wall? If you do so again, I will lay hands on you.” From that time on they did not come on the sabbath.
text.Rotherham: Therefore I testified against them, and said unto them, Wherefore are ye lodging against the wall? if ye do it again, a hand, will I thrust upon you,—From that time, they came not on the sabbath.
text.KJV: 13:21 Then I testified against them, and said vnto them, Why lodge yee about the wall? If ye doe so againe, I will lay hands on you. From that time forth came they no more on the Sabbath.
text.DRV: 13:21 And I charged them, and I said to them: Why tarry you over-against the wall? If you shall do so the second time, I will lay my hand upon you. Therefore from that time they came not on the Sabbath.

text.NRSV: And I commanded the Levites that they should purify themselves and come and guard the gates, to keep the sabbath day holy. Remember this also in my favor, O my God, and spare me according to the greatness of your steadfast love.
text.Rotherham: And I gave word to the Levites, that they should be purifying themselves and coming in, as keepers of the gates, to hallow the sabbath day. This also, remember to me, O my God, and have pity upon me, according to the abundance of thy lovingkindness.
text.KJV: 13:22 And I commanded the Leuites, that they should cleanse themselues, and that they should come and keepe the gates, to sanctifie the Sabbath day: Remember me, O my God, concerning this also, and spare me, according to the greatnesse of thy mercie.
text.DRV: 13:22 Ispake also to the Levites that they should be cleansed, and should come to keep the gates, and to sanctify the day of the Sabbath: therefore for this also remember me my God, and spare me according to the multitude of thy mercies.

text.NRSV: In those days also I saw Jews who had married women of Ashdod, Ammon, and Moab;
text.Rotherham: Moreover, in these days, saw I the Jews who had married women of Ashdod, of Ammon, of Moab;
text.KJV: 13:23 ¶ In those dayes also sawe I Iewes that had maried wiues of Ashdod, of Ammon, and of Moab:
text.DRV: 13:23 But in those days also I saw the Jews marrying wives women of Azotus, and of Ammon, and of Moab.

text.NRSV: and half of their children spoke the language of Ashdod, and they could not speak the language of Judah, but spoke the language of various peoples.
text.Rotherham: and, their children, were one-half speaking the language of Ashdod, and understood not how to speak the language of the Jews,—but after the tongue of both people.
text.KJV: 13:24 And their children spake halfe in the speech of Ashdod, and could not speake in the Iewes language, but according to the language of ech people.
text.DRV: 13:24 And their children spake, the half part the Azotian tongue, and they could not speak the Jews' language, and they spake according to the language of the people and people.

text.NRSV: And I contended with them and cursed them and beat some of them and pulled out their hair; and I made them take an oath in the name of God, saying, “You shall not give your daughters to their sons, or take their daughters for your sons or for yourselves.
text.Rotherham: So I contended with them, and laid a curse upon them, and I smote, from among them, certain men, and pulled out their hair,—and I put them on oath by God, Ye shall not give your daughters unto their sons, nor take of their daughters, for your sons nor for yourselves.
text.KJV: 13:25 And I contended with them, and cursed them, and smote certeine of them, and pluckt off their haire, and made them sweare by God, saying, Yee shall not giue your daughters vnto their sonnes, nor take their daughters vnto your sonnes, or for your selues.
text.DRV: 13:25 And I rebuked them, and cursed them. And I beat of them some men, & shaved them bald, & adjured them by God, that they should not give their daughters to their sons, nor take their daughters for their sons, and for themselves, saying:

text.NRSV: Did not King Solomon of Israel sin on account of such women? Among the many nations there was no king like him, and he was beloved by his God, and God made him king over all Israel; nevertheless, foreign women made even him to sin.
text.Rotherham: Was it not, over these things, that Solomon king of Israel sinned—though, among many nations, there was no king such as he, and he was, beloved by his God, and so God gave him to be king over all Israel,—even him, did foreign women, cause to sin.
text.KJV: 13:26 Did not Solomon king of Israel sinne by these things? yet among many nations was there no king like him, who was beloued of his God, and God made him king ouer all Israel: neuerthelesse, euen him did outlandish women cause to sinne.
text.DRV: 13:26 Did not Salomon the king of Israel sin in this kind of thing? And surely in many nations, there was not a king like to him, and he was beloved of his God, and God set him king over all Israel: him therefore also foreign women brought to sin.

text.NRSV: Shall we then listen to you and do all this great evil and act treacherously against our God by marrying foreign women?”
text.Rotherham: Unto you, then, shall we hearken, to do all this great wickedness, to act unfaithfully with our God,—by marrying foreign wives?
text.KJV: 13:27 Shall wee then hearken vnto you, to doe all this great euill, to transgresse against our God, in marrying strange wiues?
text.DRV: 13:27 And shall we also being disobedient persons do all this great evil, to transgress against our God, and to marry foreign women?

text.NRSV: And one of the sons of Jehoiada, son of the high priest Eliashib, was the son-in-law of Sanballat the Horonite; I chased him away from me.
text.Rotherham: And, one of the sons of Joiada, son of Eliashib the high priest, was son-in-law to Sanballat the Horonite,—therefore I chased him from me.
text.KJV: 13:28 And one of the sonnes of Ioiada, the sonne of Eliashib the high Priest, was sonne in law to Sanballat the Horonite: therfore I chased him from me.
text.DRV: 13:28 And Sanaballat the Horonite was son in law to one of the sons of Joiada, the son of Eliasib the high Priest, whom I drave from me.

text.NRSV: Remember them, O my God, because they have defiled the priesthood, the covenant of the priests and the Levites.
text.Rotherham: Remember them, O my God,—on account of the Defilings of the Priesthood, and the Covenant of the Priesthood, and of the Levites.
text.KJV: 13:29 Remember them, O my God, because they haue defiled the Priesthood, and the couenant of the Priesthood, and of the Leuites.
text.DRV: 13:29 Remember o Lord my God against them, that pollute the Priesthood, and the right of Priests and Levites.

text.NRSV: Thus I cleansed them from everything foreign, and I established the duties of the priests and Levites, each in his work;
text.Rotherham: So I purified them from everything foreign,—and appointed charges unto the priests and unto the Levites, every one in his work;
text.KJV: 13:30 Thus cleansed I them from all strangers, and appointed the wards of the Priests and the Leuites, euery one in his businesse:
text.DRV: 13:30 Therefore I separated from them all strangers, & I appointed the courses of the Priests and Levites, every man in his ministry:

text.NRSV: and I provided for the wood offering, at appointed times, and for the first fruits. Remember me, O my God, for good.
text.Rotherham: also for the offering of wood, at times appointed, and for the firstfruits. Remember me, O my God, for good!
text.KJV: 13:31 And for the wood-offering, at times appointed, and for the first fruits. Remember me, O my God, for good.
text.DRV: 13:31 and in the oblation of wood at times appointed, and in the first fruits: Remember me my God unto good. Amen.
